title: Fix cross-device messaging: replace BroadcastChannel with backend-persisted messages

requirements.text: Implement a backend-persisted message store in `backend/main.mo`. Add a stable array of chat messages (storing id, username, text, timestamp, and all existing ChatMessage fields: isBigMessage, isForced, isBot, isSystem, isBroadcast). Expose Motoko query and update functions: `getMessages(since: Nat) : async [ChatMessage]` to fetch messages newer than a given timestamp, and `postMessage(msg: ChatMessage) : async ()` to store a new message (cap the store at 500 most recent messages).

quotes: after publishing i opened in my mobile and said something but in pc that message is not showing

acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo defines a stable var for chat message storage
getMessages query accepts a since timestamp and returns only messages newer than it
postMessage update function appends a message to stable storage
Message store is capped at 500 entries (oldest pruned first)
All fields of the ChatMessage interface (isBigMessage, isForced, isBot, isSystem, etc.) are represented in the Motoko record type

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/hooks/useBroadcastMessages.ts` to use the ICP backend as the primary message store. On mount and on a polling interval (every 2 seconds), call `getMessages(since)` on the backend actor to fetch new messages and merge them into local React state. When sending a message, call `postMessage` on the backend actor to persist it, in addition to (or instead of) writing to localStorage. Continue broadcasting via BroadcastChannel for same-browser tab sync as a secondary optimization, but the backend must be the source of truth for cross-device delivery.

quotes: after publishing i opened in my mobile and said something but in pc that message is not showing

acceptanceCriteria: Messages posted from a mobile device appear on a PC browser within ~2 seconds
Messages posted on PC appear on mobile within ~2 seconds
Existing BroadcastChannel behavior is preserved for same-browser tab sync
No duplicate messages appear in the chat when both BroadcastChannel and polling deliver the same message (deduplicate by message id)
localStorage is still updated for offline/fallback display on reload

requirements.text: Ensure the `ChatMessage` TypeScript interface in `frontend/src/types/chat.ts` has an `id` field (string, required) that is unique per message (e.g., a UUID or timestamp+random string). All message creation points (user sends, bot responses, admin broadcasts, force text, big messages, system messages) must generate and assign a unique `id` before persisting. This id is used for deduplication when polling from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatMessage interface includes `id: string` as a required field
Every message sent (user, bot, admin, system) has a unique non-empty id assigned at creation time
Polling deduplication logic uses the id field to avoid showing the same message twice

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
BroadcastChannel + localStorage behavior must be preserved as a secondary same-browser sync layer — do not remove it
Polling interval must not be lower than 1 second to avoid excessive canister calls
nonGoals: WebSockets or real-time push from backend to frontend
Changing the visual design or layout of the chat UI
Modifying authentication or admin panel logic
Migrating existing localStorage message history to the backend on first load
